2026-07-05.topic: Extreme ironing is a sport in which people take ironing boards to remote locations and iron items of clothing. This bizarre sport originated in England.
A person could survive in space without a spacesuit, but only for 10–15 seconds before losing consciousness due to a lack of oxygen.
Llamas are sometimes used as guard animals for other livestock, bonding with sheep or goats and deterring predators by barking, spitting, or charging.
Fiji has two native frogs, the Fiji tree frog and the Fiji ground frog.
In 2010, a man successfully robbed a Long Island bank wearing a cape and a Darth Vader mask.
Coco Chanel’s favorite number was the number five. She thought it brought her luck, so she released Chanel No. 5 on the fifth day of May, the fifth month of the year.
Abebe Bikila won Ethiopia’s first Olympic gold in the 1960 Rome Olympic marathon, famously running barefoot. Nine years later, he was involved in a car accident that left him paralyzed.
Aerophobia is the irrational fear of air, and sufferers may avoid going outside, opening windows, or being near people using fans, air conditioners, or hair dryers.
Sunday is the second day of the week in the old Persian calendar, used in Afghanistan and Iran. Funnily enough, it’s called day one, as their week begins on day zero, Saturday.
The Great Wall of China has stood for centuries due to a unique mortar mix of sticky rice flour and slaked lime.
In aerial bonding or territorial displays between pairs, Eastern Imperial Eagles may engage in “cartwheeling,” locking talons, and rolling in flight closely toward the ground.
July 5 is Mechanical Pencil Day, a day that celebrates the convenient mechanical pencil and encourages people to write by hand or explore its history.
In 1900, physician Jesse William Lazear deliberately allowed an infected mosquito to bite him to prove that yellow fever was spread by mosquitoes. He died from yellow fever at age 34.
Uganda has the highest banana consumption per capita in the world, with the average Ugandan consuming between 1.1 and 2.2 pounds (500–1,000 grams) per day.
More than 100 million bicycles, including e-bikes, are produced worldwide each year, with China accounting for about 65% of total production.
The Moon’s gravity drives most of Earth’s tides, but the Sun’s gravitational pull also contributes significantly.
A pentagon is a shape with 5 sides, and it’s used in the design of the Pentagon building in the United States. It also appears in old-style soccer balls and some flower shapes.
The small pieces of paper produced by a hole punch are known as chad.
The Moon is in its Waning Gibbous phase tonight, with roughly 75% illumination, the stage after Full Moon when the lit portion begins to decrease.
American actor Jason Dolley is celebrating his 35th birthday today. He was born on July 5, 1991 in California, USA, making his zodiac sign Cancer.
July is one of the most common months for reports of crop circles in cornfields, and marks the point when farmers hope their corn is “knee-high by the Fourth of July.”
Though abolished in 1865, slavery-like conditions continued in the U.S. up until at least the 1960s through peonage, an illegal system that was effectively no different from slavery.
Crocodiles can live for over 100 years in captivity. In the wild, species such as saltwater, Nile, and American crocodiles typically live 50-70 years, with exceptional cases nearing 100.
“Rubber Duckie,” a song performed by Ernie on Sesame Street, became a real-world hit in 1970 and reached No. 16 on the Billboard Hot 100.
“Gentle Johnny” Ramensky was a nonviolent career criminal who became a British Commando during WWII due to his safe-cracking skills.
Rare unopened LEGO sets have at times outperformed stocks, bonds, and even gold in returns.
A quintillion is written as 1 followed by 18 zeros. Forty-three quintillion is the number of possible ways all the different colored pieces of a Rubik’s Cube can be arranged.
Sea snakes hydrate by drinking from a freshwater layer that forms on the ocean’s surface after rainfall.
Belarusians consume more potatoes per capita than anyone else in the world, with the average person in Belarus eating 341-375 pounds (155-170 kg) every year.
All new FBI special agents and intelligence analysts must visit the U.S. Holocaust Memorial Museum as part of their training.

2026-07-06.topic: When the historic Plaza Hotel in New York City opened in 1907, a room cost $2.50 per night, which is equivalent to around $65 today. The Plaza now charges over $1,000 per night.
In 2013, over 200,000 people applied for Mars One’s one-way mission to Mars, but the project was later canceled after going bankrupt in 2019.
The blue-eyed black lemur is the only primate besides humans with naturally blue eyes, making it unique among more than 500 primate species.
Cuba is home to the world’s smallest bird, the bee hummingbird. Measuring up to 2.4 inches (6.1 cm) long, they weigh just 0.09 ounces (2.6 g)!
As of 2025, the U.S. had the most billionaires, with 902 people reaching at least a ten-digit figure!
The number six is the first perfect number, or a positive number whose value is reached by adding up all the numbers that can divide it evenly. In this case, 1+2+3=6.
John Cabot, the 15th-century explorer known for the first European voyage to North America since the Vikings, was born in Italy and originally named Giovanni Caboto.
Agoraphobia is the fear of being in a public place, and long-term use of tranquilizers, sleeping pills, alcohol, or tobacco may contribute to its development.
Monday is the only day of the week with a valid English anagram, where its letters can be rearranged to spell the word “dynamo.”
Chewing gum can temporarily boost memory and attention, likely due to increased brain stimulation.
Great auks, a large, flightless relative of puffins, were driven to extinction by 1844 as their downy feathers were prized for making pillows.
July 6 is Virtually Hug a Virtual Assistant Day, a day that appreciates virtual assistants by encouraging people to send a virtual hug via text, email, or video call.
Sean Connery was once stopped for speeding by an officer named Sergeant James Bond.
The population of Ireland is still about one million lower than it was before the Great Famine of the 1840s, a period when potato crop failures led to mass starvation and emigration.
The first piano was created in 1700 by an Italian called Bartolomeo di Francesco Cristofori.
In the 18th century, French astronomer Charles Messier cataloged 110 of the most fascinating astronomical objects he found while searching the night sky for comets.
A hexagon is a shape with 6 sides, and bees use it to build honeycombs because it fills space without leaving gaps. It’s also used in tile patterns and in the structure of snowflakes.
In 2018, an elevator with six people fell 84 floors in a Chicago skyscraper. Miraculously, every single person survived, and no one required hospitalization!
The Moon is in its Waning Gibbous phase tonight, with roughly 66% illumination, the stage after Full Moon when the lit portion begins to decrease.
American comedian Kevin Hart is celebrating his 47th birthday today. He was born on July 6, 1979 in Pennsylvania, USA, making his zodiac sign Cancer.
July is one of the most common months for reports of crop circles in cornfields, and marks the point when farmers hope their corn is “knee-high by the Fourth of July.”
In 2012, Domino’s in the Netherlands used silent electric scooters for deliveries. They added a human voice making “vroom” sounds and saying “Domino’s Pizza” to alert people nearby.
Badgers have occasionally been observed behaving drunkenly after consuming fermented or rotting fruit, which contains ethanol.
Developed in the 1910s, the modern zipper was first used on boots and tobacco pouches before spreading to children’s clothing and men’s trousers in the 1930s.
Pope Fabian, who served from 236 to 250 AD, was appointed to his position because a dove landed on his head during the election. He wasn’t even a candidate.
In 2014, the Fresno chapter of the Hells Angels waited in line for five days at a Walmart to purchase over 200 bicycles, which they donated to children in need.
A sextillion is 10²¹, or a 1 followed by 21 zeros, and is sometimes used to estimate the number of grains of sand across all of the world’s beaches.
On Disney’s Space Mountain, fans blow air on riders to make the roller coaster feel faster than it really is.
In Ethiopia, Orthodox Christians celebrate Ganna (their Christmas) on January 7, after a 43-day fast, marking the day with church, family gatherings, and a spicy dish of doro wat stew.
Using a hands-free device while driving is not significantly safer than using a handheld device, as both cause cognitive distractions that can lead to serious accidents.

2026-07-08.topic: The word “kimono” literally translates to “a thing to wear” in Japanese. It is composed of the kanji characters 着 (ki), which means “to wear,” and 物 (mono), which means “thing.”
Pluto was named by eleven-year-old Venetia Burney from England, who suggested the name after the Roman god of the underworld.
Jungle cats are one of the few wild cats that don’t just swim, but actually seem to enjoy being in water and will dive in to catch fish.
The Archbishop of Cyprus is allowed to sign official papers with vermilion ink, a reddish-orange color made using cinnabar.
The trams in Hiroshima were back in service just three days after the U.S. bombed the city in 1945.
The number eight is the luckiest number in Chinese culture, as it sounds very similar to the word for wealth or prosperity.
Häagen-Dazs is an American brand. Founded by Reuben and Rose Mattus in the Bronx, New York, in 1960, they just thought the name sounded premium.
Ailurophobia is the irrational fear of cats. In Minecraft, explosive Creepers have it, and flee from cats within a six-block radius, despite those animals posing no threat to them.
In Irish and Scottish Gaelic, the word for Wednesday means “first day of fasting,” coming from the words “chéad” for first and “aoine” for fasting.
Rocks in streams and rivers create vortices that can allow a dead fish to “swim” upstream.
Unlike most geese, nēnē evolved to be more terrestrial. With stronger legs and much less webbing on their toes, they can walk greater distances on land, even across lava flows.
July 8 is Math 2.0 Day, a day that celebrates the fusion of mathematics and technology and encourages people to solve math puzzles or explore digital innovations.
J. R. R. Tolkien started writing a sequel to The Lord of the Rings called “The New Shadow,” but abandoned it after 13 pages because he found it too depressing.
Guam, a Micronesian U.S. territory, is fourteen to fifteen hours (daylight saving time dependent) ahead of New York, meaning it gets movie releases before anyone else in the States.
Research indicates that schizophrenic hallucinations vary culturally; in the U.S., voices are often harsh, whereas in India and Ghana, they tend to be more playful or positive.
Every 26 seconds, Earth gives off a faint seismic pulse, and scientists still aren’t sure what causes it.
An octagon is a shape with 8 sides, and it’s used for stop signs in many countries. The shape makes the sign instantly recognizable, even from a distance or in poor visibility.
In May 2003, a Boeing 727 was stolen from Quatro de Fevereiro Airport in Luanda, Angola. Neither the plane nor the thieves were ever seen again.
Tonight, Wednesday, July 8, 2026, the Moon is in its Last Quarter phase, meaning it has reached the halfway point between Full Moon and New Moon.
American actor Jaden Smith is celebrating his 28th birthday today. He was born on July 8, 1998 in California, USA, making his zodiac sign Cancer.
July is one of the most common months for reports of crop circles in cornfields, and marks the point when farmers hope their corn is “knee-high by the Fourth of July.”
Humans are the only animals on the planet that cry due to emotional stress. All other animals simply cry to lubricate their eyes.
Armadillos typically rest for about 16 hours each day, with some species, like the giant armadillo, known to sleep even longer.
A female gladiator in ancient Rome was called a gladiatrix, but women’s participation in arena combat was rare and outlawed after AD 200.
Taking vitamin B6 supplements before bed can enhance your ability to recall dreams.
In the Titanic movie, crew member William Murdoch shot a passenger and then took his own life. In real life, he went down with the ship while filling lifeboats and saving lives.
An octillion is a 1 followed by 27 zeros, and for scale, the human body contains an estimated seven octillion atoms.
The average American household debt reached around $105,000 in 2024, marking a 13% increase since 2020.
The earliest written evidence of coffee’s consumption and cultivation dates back to the 15th century in Yemen.
Staying up all night can sometimes improve mood in depression. This is because not sleeping boosts brain activity and increases dopamine, a mood-enhancing chemical.
